utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all five of these items appear. Each one is fully specified with type, coverage description, features, and detailed cost information (monthly and annual). The total_budget section includes complete cost breakdown and relevant compliance information. Therefore, all required items are included and fully detailed.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes four key components: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, and additional_services. Each component uses the 'LifeFirst Comprehensive Bundle' branding. In the ground-truth ratings, there are 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' entries under health and travel, and 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' under property. Since the bundle is composed of these LifeFirst standard components, we can map each part accordingly. The additional services (Enhanced Support Services) correspond most closely to 24/7 customer support and emergency coordination, which align with 'Telemedicine Services' (rating 10) and essential assistance features, but since it’s bundled and not separately rated, no explicit rating exists in the dataset. Therefore, we map the main three coverage components to their known LifeFirst counterparts and include only those with defined ratings from the ground-truth data.
